frontier_title: Cosmos 3: Omnimodal World Models for Physical AI

residual_gaps.gap_text: The mixture-of-transformers architecture inherits the mixture-of-experts (MoE) paradigm without a mechanism to control inference latency under sparse routing, failing to establish 'inference efficiency under varying expert activation'.
residual_gaps.ancestor_assumption_blocking_it: Qwen3-VL Technical Report assumes that MoE with interleaved-MRoPE and DeepStack provides sufficient efficiency without addressing token-level routing overhead.
residual_gaps.why_evolution_did_not_close_it: The structural evolution action replaced modality-specific components with a unified MoT but kept the MoE paradigm untouched; no new routing sparsity control or expert fusion method was introduced to reduce inference latency.
residual_gaps.why_not_resolved: The frontier retained the same architectural paradigm (MoE) from Qwen3-VL, so the inherent latency trade-offs remained unaddressed.

residual_gaps.gap_text: The unified model still relies on large-scale curated synthetic multimodal data for training, lacking a mechanism for 'data-efficient adaptation' from few examples or self-supervised signals.
residual_gaps.ancestor_assumption_blocking_it: Qwen-VL: A Versatile Vision-Language Model for Understanding (background) assumes a 3-stage training pipeline with large-scale curated image-text pairs is necessary for vision-language alignment.
residual_gaps.why_evolution_did_not_close_it: The structural evolution action unified multiple modalities into a single backbone but did not change the training data assumption; it still requires large-scale curated synthetic videos, images, and audio, rather than introducing self-supervised or few-shot adaptation strategies.
residual_gaps.why_not_resolved: The frontier did not challenge the reliance on large curated datasets inherited from Qwen-VL, leaving data efficiency unresolved.

residual_gaps.gap_text: The model is trained on static offline datasets and evaluated on generation benchmarks, failing to establish 'closed-loop interactive learning' from environment feedback or task execution.
residual_gaps.ancestor_assumption_blocking_it: Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding  (background) assumes representation learning from static images suffices for downstream tasks without online interaction.
residual_gaps.why_evolution_did_not_close_it: The structural evolution action extended predictive masking to multiple modalities but retained offline static dataset training; the evaluation benchmarks (Text-to-Image, Image-to-Video) are one-shot generation tasks, not interactive embodied agent scenarios.
residual_gaps.why_not_resolved: The frontier kept the offline static training paradigm from I-JEPA, so the model does not learn from closed-loop feedback or adapt to dynamic environments.

shared_residual_gaps.gap_text: Across branches B and C, the assumption that large static curated datasets are sufficient for training persists, leaving 'data efficiency for physical AI tasks' unestablished; the frontier still relies on curated synthetic data from B and static offline data from C.
shared_residual_gaps.structural_property_name: data efficiency for physical AI tasks
shared_residual_gaps.common_assumption: Both Qwen-VL: A Versatile Vision-Language Model for Understanding (background) and Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding  (background) assume that offline static training on curated datasets yields representations general enough for embodied tasks.

shared_residual_gaps.gap_text: Branches B and C both evaluate on static benchmarks without interactive feedback, and the frontier similarly uses generation metrics, failing to establish 'closed-loop task-level performance measurement' for embodied agents.
shared_residual_gaps.common_assumption: Both Qwen-VL (background) and Self-Supervised Learning from Images (background) assume that offline benchmark scores correlate with real-world deployment performance, ignoring the need for interactive evaluation.

recent_actions.what_changed: A replaces B's modality-specific components (interleaved-MRoPE, DeepStack, MoE for vision-language) with a unified mixture-of-transformers that jointly represents and generates across five modalities.

recent_actions.from_title: Qwen-VL: A Versatile Vision-Language Model for Understanding, Localization, Text Reading, and Beyond (background)
recent_actions.what_changed: A replaces Qwen-VL's vision-specific visual receptor and 3-stage training pipeline with a single mixture-of-transformers backbone trained on omnimodal data for flexible understanding and generation.

recent_actions.from_title: Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (background)
recent_actions.what_changed: A replaces B's single-modality predictive block masking with a mixture-of-transformers that jointly embeds and predicts across modalities.

frontier_solves: The primary contribution is the development of Cosmos 3, a family of omnimodal world models built on a mixture-of-transformers architecture. It seamlessly integrates understanding and generation tasks for language, image, video, audio, and action, providing a scalable, general-purpose backbone for embodied agents.
frontier_method: Cosmos 3 employs a mixture-of-transformers architecture that processes multiple modalities in a unified manner. The model is trained on large-scale multimodal data, enabling flexible input-output configurations. Key design choices include joint representation learning across modalities and post-trai
frontier_what_persists: A inherits the mixture-of-experts architectural paradigm from B, including its associated inference latency and potential quality trade-offs.
A inherits B's reliance on large-scale curated training data and does not explore self-supervised or few-shot adaptation to reduce data dependence.
A inherits B's assumption that representation learning can be done offline on static datasets, without needing online interaction or causal feedback.
